name: Dependency Graph Resolver with Cycles
description: Implement a dependency resolver across two modules. Module dep_types.py must define: Node dataclass with fields name (str), dependencies (list[str], default empty), data (Any, default None); CycleError exception with attribute cycle (list[str]); ResolutionPlan dataclass with fields order (list[str], topological order) and parallel_groups (list[list[str]], where each group can be processed in parallel after previous groups). Module dep_resolver.py must import from dep_types.py and implement Graph class with methods: add_node(node: Node); remove_node(name: str); add_edge(from_name: str, to_name: str); resolve(target: str | None = None) returns ResolutionPlan, raising CycleError on cycles. If target is None, plan covers all nodes; otherwise only target plus its transitive dependencies. parallel_groups: group N contains all nodes whose dependencies are all in groups < N.
test_imports: from dep_types import Node, CycleError, ResolutionPlan
signature:
# dep_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

@dataclass
class Node:
    name: str
    dependencies: list = field(default_factory=list)
    data: Any = None

class CycleError(Exception):
    def __init__(self, cycle: list):
        super().__init__(f'cycle detected: {cycle}')
        self.cycle = cycle

@dataclass
class ResolutionPlan:
    order: list
    parallel_groups: list

# dep_resolver.py
from dep_types import Node, CycleError, ResolutionPlan

class Graph:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def add_node(self, node: Node) -> None: ...
    def remove_node(self, name: str) -> None: ...
    def add_edge(self, from_name: str, to_name: str) -> None: ...
    def resolve(self, target: str | None = None) -> ResolutionPlan: ...
tests:
def test_single_node():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert plan.order == ['a']
    assert plan.parallel_groups == [['a']]

def test_linear_chain():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('c', dependencies=['b']))
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert plan.order == ['a', 'b', 'c']

def test_parallel_independent():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b'))
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert sorted(plan.parallel_groups[0]) == ['a', 'b']

def test_diamond():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('c', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('d', dependencies=['b', 'c']))
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert plan.order[0] == 'a'
    assert plan.order[-1] == 'd'
    assert set(plan.order[1:3]) == {'b', 'c'}

def test_target_subgraph():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('c'))
    plan = g.resolve(target='b')
    assert set(plan.order) == {'a', 'b'}

def test_remove_node():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b', dependencies=['a']))
    g.remove_node('b')
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert plan.order == ['a']

def test_add_edge():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b'))
    g.add_edge('b', 'a')
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert plan.order == ['a', 'b']

def test_parallel_groups_diamond():
    g = Graph()
    g.add_node(Node('a'))
    g.add_node(Node('b', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('c', dependencies=['a']))
    g.add_node(Node('d', dependencies=['b', 'c']))
    plan = g.resolve()
    assert len(plan.parallel_groups) == 3
    assert plan.parallel_groups[0] == ['a']
    assert sorted(plan.parallel_groups[1]) == ['b', 'c']
    assert plan.parallel_groups[2] == ['d']
constraints: Add a node before adding edges referencing it
Cycles must be detected and raise CycleError, not infinite loop
Topological order is one valid sort (any valid order acceptable for tests using set comparison)
parallel_groups[i] contains nodes whose deps are all in groups[0..i-1]
Removing a node also removes references to it from other nodes' dependency lists
resolve(target=X) limits result to X and its transitive dependencies
